{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八柱神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[碧南市]][[弥生町 (碧南市)|弥生町]]3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 52|緯度秒 = 07.9 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 20.0 |祭神 = [[アメノオシホミミ|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命]]
[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = 仁寿3年（853年）8月21日 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''八柱神社'''（やはしらじんしゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[碧南市]][[弥生町 (碧南市)|弥生町]]3-140にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。仁寿3年（853年）8月21日創建。[[碧海郡]][[棚尾町]]の氏神だった。境外社として、1931年（昭和6年）に創建された[[護国神社]]の[[棚尾神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[アメノオシホミミ|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命]]（あめのおしほみみのみこと） * [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]（あまつひこねのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|境外社の[[棚尾神社]]]] 仁寿3年（853年）8月21日、志貴荘司の志貴左衛門藤原周亮が[[八王子宮]]を創建した[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm 八柱神社] トボトボ歩く碧南市。永歴元年（1160年）、[[尾張国|尾張]][[知多郡]][[内海町 (愛知県)|内海]]から来た長田白正が神主となり、以後は[[明治維新]]まで長田氏が社家を務めた。現在の大浜地区にある[[西方寺 (碧南市)|西方寺]]は、明応5年（1496年）まで八王子宮の境内にあり、光照寺と称していた。 文政10年（1827年）には、前浜新田を開発した斎藤倭助によって[[灯籠]]が建立された。1872年（明治5年）9月には八王子宮から八柱神社に改称し、[[近代社格制度]]における村社となった。1884年（明治17年）2月28日には郷社に昇格した。同年3月18日には書家の[[山中信天翁]]が社号額を奉納している。 1919年（大正8年）9月には、[[矢作川]]に架かる棚尾橋の償却期終了記念として、境内に太鼓橋が建立された。1923年（大正12年）には大[[鳥居]]が建立された。1931年（昭和6年）10月18日には、境外社として[[護国神社]]の[[棚尾神社]]が創建された[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf 「棚尾神社と忠魂碑] 棚尾の歴史を語る会。八柱神社近辺には造り酒屋の旦那衆に支えられて花街が形成されており、劇場[[三栄座]]の開館によってこの花街も発展した。 == 地理 == かつての[[碧海郡]][[棚尾町]]の集落の南端部にある。かつては[[碧海台地]]南西部の突端部に位置しており、神社の南側や西側には海（[[衣浦湾]]）が広がっていた。海岸には文禄年間（1592年-1596年）以後に[[塩田]]が形成され、塩浜町という町名に名残が残っている。ここで生産された塩は大浜塩として信濃国に運ばれ、[[にがり]]成分が多い特徴を生かして塩漬けなどに使用された。境内には井戸「弥生の井」があり、[[醸造業]]に適する水質だったことから、棚尾では醸造業が栄えた。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町3-140 ; 交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[名鉄三河線]][[碧南駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 碧南事典編さん会「八柱神社」『碧南事典』碧南市、1993年、469頁 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ 八柱神社] 碧南観光ナビ * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm 八柱神社] トボトボ歩く碧南市 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やはしらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:碧南市の歴史]] [[Category:碧南市の建築物]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 八柱神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[碧南市]][[弥生町 (碧南市)|弥生町]]3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 52|緯度秒 = 07.9 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 20.0 |祭神 = [[アメノオシホミミ|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命]]
[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = 仁寿3年（853年）8月21日 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''八柱神社'''（やはしらじんしゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[碧南市]][[弥生町 (碧南市)|弥生町]]3-140にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。仁寿3年（853年）8月21日創建。[[碧海郡]][[棚尾町]]の氏神だった。境外社として、1931年（昭和6年）に創建された[[護国神社]]の[[棚尾神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[アメノオシホミミ|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命]]（あめのおしほみみのみこと） * [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]（あまつひこねのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|境外社の[[棚尾神社]]]] 仁寿3年（853年）8月21日、志貴荘司の志貴左衛門藤原周亮が[[八王子宮]]を創建した[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm 八柱神社] トボトボ歩く碧南市。永歴元年（1160年）、[[尾張国|尾張]][[知多郡]][[内海町 (愛知県)|内海]]から来た長田白正が神主となり、以後は[[明治維新]]まで長田氏が社家を務めた。現在の大浜地区にある[[西方寺 (碧南市)|西方寺]]は、明応5年（1496年）まで八王子宮の境内にあり、光照寺と称していた。 文政10年（1827年）には、前浜新田を開発した斎藤倭助によって[[灯籠]]が建立された。1872年（明治5年）9月には八王子宮から八柱神社に改称し、[[近代社格制度]]における村社となった。1884年（明治17年）2月28日には郷社に昇格した。同年3月18日には書家の[[山中信天翁]]が社号額を奉納している。 1919年（大正8年）9月には、[[矢作川]]に架かる棚尾橋の償却期終了記念として、境内に太鼓橋が建立された。1923年（大正12年）には大[[鳥居]]が建立された。1931年（昭和6年）10月18日には、境外社として[[護国神社]]の[[棚尾神社]]が創建された[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf 「棚尾神社と忠魂碑] 棚尾の歴史を語る会。八柱神社近辺には造り酒屋の旦那衆に支えられて花街が形成されており、劇場[[三栄座]]の開館によってこの花街も発展した。 == 地理 == かつての[[碧海郡]][[棚尾町]]の集落の南端部にある。かつては[[碧海台地]]南西部の突端部に位置しており、神社の南側や西側には海（[[衣浦湾]]）が広がっていた。海岸には文禄年間（1592年-1596年）以後に[[塩田]]が形成され、塩浜町という町名に名残が残っている。ここで生産された塩は大浜塩として信濃国に運ばれ、[[にがり]]成分が多い特徴を生かして塩漬けなどに使用された。境内には井戸「弥生の井」があり、[[醸造業]]に適する水質だったことから、棚尾では醸造業が栄えた。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町3-140 ; 交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[名鉄三河線]][[碧南駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 碧南事典編さん会「八柱神社」『碧南事典』碧南市、1993年、469頁 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ 八柱神社] 碧南観光ナビ * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm 八柱神社] トボトボ歩く碧南市 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やはしらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:碧南市の歴史]] [[Category:碧南市の建築物]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gōsha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hekinan]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]]

The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hekinan]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Japan]] }} [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gō-sha |founded = {{ill|Ninju 3|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hekinan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines in Japan]] }} [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]]

Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai District|qq=}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}]]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|qq=}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chita District|qq=}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|qq=}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|qq=}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahagi River|qq=}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai District|qq=}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|qq=}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|qq=}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Mikawa Line|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hekinan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] }} [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]]

In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|qq=}} in {{ill|Chita District|ja|知多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chita District|qq=}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}}, became the head priest. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai District|qq=}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|qq=}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|qq=}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Mikawa Line|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hekinan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] }} [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)]] [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], [[Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = [[Amenooshihomimi]] (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hekinan, Aichi|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan, Aichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|qq=}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|qq=}} in {{ill|Chita District, Aichi|ja|知多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chita District, Aichi|qq=}}, became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|qq=}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|qq=}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]][[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|ja|矢作川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahagi River|qq=}}. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|qq=}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|qq=}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|qq=}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|qq=}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|ja|名鉄三河線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Mikawa Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|ja|碧南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan City)]] [[Category:kkk]] }} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan City)]] [[sn:Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan City)]] [[sn:ahashira Shrine (Hekinan City)]] [[Category:Merged pages]]
