{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gō-sha |founded = [[:en:Ninju|Ninju 3]] (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi |location = {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.86886111111111|N|136.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in [[:en:Hekikai District|Hekikai District]]. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in [[:en:Chita District|Chita District]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]], became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the [[:en:Yahagi River|Yahagi River]]. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in [[:en:Hekikai District|Hekikai District]], near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hekinan Station|Hekinan Station]] on the [[:en:Meitetsu Mikawa Line|Meitetsu Mikawa Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Amenooshihomimi]] (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * [[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in [[:en:Chita District|Chita District, Aichi]], became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from [[:en:Meitetsu Mikawa Line|Meitetsu Mikawa Line]] [[:en:Hekinan Station|Hekinan Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the [[:en:Yahagi River|Yahagi River]] [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)

The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Aichi}}, became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}} [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86194281]] Wikidata name: Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja 2022-01 ac (2).jpg |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gō-sha |founded = {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju 3}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi |location = {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.86886111111111|N|136.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Aichi}}, became the chief priest. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}} [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86194281]] Wikidata name: Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja 2022-01 ac (2).jpg |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gō-sha |founded = {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju 3}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi |location = {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.86886111111111|N|136.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Aichi}}, became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}} [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Aichi}}, became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). After the Bunroku era (1592–1596), salt fields were developed along the coastline, giving rise to the name Shiohama Town. The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}} [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)]][[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja 2022-01 ac (2).jpg |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi-chō (Hekinan)|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}} 3-140 |ISO = JP-23 |latitude = 34|LatMin = 52|LatSec = 07.9 |longitude = 136|LonMin = 59|LonSec = 20.0 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} |SacredObjects = |type= Former Gō-sha |founded = {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju 3}} (853), August 21 |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |festival = Third Sunday in October |Rites = |map = Japan Aichi |location = {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.86886111111111|N|136.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. consecration_year on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), it served as the guardian shrine of the Tanao community in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to the spirits of war dead, was established in 1931. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Tanao Gokoku Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}}]] The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Ninju 3), by Shiki Saemon Fujiwara Norisuke, who constructed the {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} on the site.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine], "Walking Around Hekinan City." In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Shosei from {{ill|Utsumi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District}}, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}}, became the head priest. The Nagata family served as shrine priests until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The nearby {{ill|Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihō-ji Temple (Hekinan)|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}}, originally located within the shrine grounds, was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). Other historical landmarks include a stone lantern erected in 1827 (Bunsei 10) by Saitō Wasuke, who developed the Maehama New Fields. The shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine in 1872 (Meiji 5) and elevated to Gōsha Rank in 1884 (Meiji 17). In the same year, a shrine plaque was donated by the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shintenō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shintenō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}}. In 1919 (Taishō 8), a taiko bridge was built to commemorate the repayment of the debt for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}}. Other notable additions include a large torii in 1923 (Taishō 12) and the establishment of Tanao Shrine as an auxiliary shrine in 1931 (Shōwa 6).[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Monument to Loyal Spirits"], "Telling the History of Tanao." == Geography == The shrine is situated in the southern part of the former Tanao village in {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|WD=Q5368803|lt=Hekikai District}}, near the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}. Historically, it overlooked the sea, with {{ill|Enokura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enokura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}} stretching to its south and west. Salt fields were developed along the coast during the Bunroku era (1592–1596), leaving the name Shiobama-chō ("Salt Beach Town") as a legacy. Salt produced here was exported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used for pickling, thanks to its high brine content. The shrine grounds include a well known as "Yayoi Well," whose water quality contributed to the flourishing of the brewing industry in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; Address * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access * By train: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hekinan City Encyclopedia Committee, "Yahashira Shrine," ''Hekinan Encyclopedia,'' Hekinan City, 1993, p. 469. == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Walking Around Hekinan City {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八柱神社 (碧南市)]] [[ja:八柱神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building | name = Yahashira Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao Yahashira-jinja ac (1).jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hekinan, Aichi|Hekinan]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|52|07.9|N|136|59|20.0|E}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命)
[[Amatsuhikone]] (天津彦根命) | established = August 21, 853 (Nijū 3) | former status = [[Gōsha]] | annual festival = Third Sunday of October | map = Japan Aichi }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine was established on August 21, 853 (Nijū 3). It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi}} (正哉吾勝勝速日天忍穂耳命) * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone}} (天津彦根命) == History == [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|thumb|Tanao Shrine, the associated shrine]] Yahashira Shrine was founded on August 21, 853, by Fujiwara no Suzunari, the Shiki Sōji, under the name Hachioji-no-miya.[http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi In 1160 (Eiryaku 1), Nagata Hakusho, who had come from {{ill|Utsumi, Aichi|ja|内海町 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsumi, Aichi|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394137}} in {{ill|Chita District|en|Chita District|WD=Q767312|lt=Chita District, Aichi}}, became the chief priest. The Nagata family continued as shrine custodians until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. The present-day {{ill|Saifō-ji Temple|ja|西方寺 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifō-ji Temple|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65282046}} was originally located within the shrine grounds and was known as Kōshō-ji until 1496 (Meiō 5). In 1827 (Bunsei 10), Saitō Wasuke, who developed Maehama Shinden, erected a stone lantern at the shrine. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was renamed Yahashira Shrine from Hachioji-no-miya, and it became a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. It was elevated to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status on February 28, 1884. In the same year, the calligrapher {{ill|Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja|山中信天翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Shinten'ō|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山中静逸|1=Yamanaka Seiitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466220}} donated an inscribed wooden plaque. In September 1919, a drum bridge was built in the precincts to commemorate the Completion tier 4]]. In 1923, a large torii gate was erected. On October 18, 1931, {{ill|Tanao Shrine|ja|棚尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Shrine|1=Tanao Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64226302}}, a branch shrine, was founded as a protective shrine for fallen soldiers.[http://www.city.hekinan.lg.jp/material/files/group/7/katarukai20.pdf "Tanao Shrine and the Memorial Monument"] Tanao History Discussion Group == Geography == Yahashira Shrine is located at the southern end of the former {{ill|Tanao Town|ja|棚尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanao Town|1=Tanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539002}} settlement. Historically, it was situated on the southwestern tip of the {{ill|Hekikai Plateau|ja|碧海台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekikai Plateau|1=Hekikai Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30931817}}, facing the sea to the south and west (present-day {{ill|Kinuura Bay|ja|衣浦湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuura Bay|1=Kinuura Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378563}}). The locally produced salt, known as Ōhama Salt, was transported to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} and used in salt-preserving techniques due to its high brine content. A well named "Yayoi Well" remains in the shrine grounds, and its water, suitable for brewing, contributed to the prosperity of sake breweries in Tanao. == Visitor Information == ; location * 3-140 Yayoi-chō, Hekinan, Aichi Prefecture ; Access * **Rail**: 15-minute walk from {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|WD=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} {{ill|Hekinan Station|en|Hekinan Station|WD=Q11587780|lt=Hekinan Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|Hachioji Shrine=Hachioji Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=八王子神社}} * {{ill|Hekinan tourism|ja|碧南観光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekinan tourism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hekinan-kanko.jp/highlight/detail/100/ Yahashira Shrine] - Hekinan Tourism Guide * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~hiro32/machi_info/tanao_area/tanao04.htm Yahashira Shrine] - Tobotobo Aruku Hekinan-shi {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|version=100311923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:八柱神社]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%A2%A7%E5%8D%97%E5%B8%82)]] {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Hekinan)}} {{wikidata link|Q86194281}} [[Category: of the repayment term for Tanao Bridge over the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|WD=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River}} [[Category:Buildings in Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hekinan, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Hekinan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
